put.description: oila360 child device endpoint used by OilaDeviceClient.completeRecording. Path form matches the migrated Swift call `device/recordings/{id}/complete`; mirrors the legacy `/api/devices/recordings/{id}/complete`.

post.description: oila360 child device endpoint used by OilaDeviceClient.reportRemovalAttempt. Path form matches the migrated Swift call `device/apps/removal-attempt`; mirrors the legacy `/api/devices/{dsn}/applications/removal-attempt`.

schema.title: Response Get Device Global Application Lock Api Devices  Id  Global Application Lock Get

get.description:
Return current lock status based on the saved schedule and device local time.

Example: schedule 22:00-07:00 returns is_locked=true at 23:30 and false at 08:00.

Example: schedule 08:00-18:00 returns is_locked=true at 12:00 and false at 19:00.

get.summary: Unsecure Get Device Global Application Lock

schema.title: Response Unsecure Get Device Global Application Lock Api Devices Dsn  Dsn  Global Application Lock Get

get.summary: Unsecure Get Device Full Lock Status
get.description:
Same as full_lock_status by id, but for child device clients by DSN.

Example: device client polls this to decide whether to fully block the UI now.

post.summary: Report Applications Usage (live oila360 device endpoint)
post.description: Live api.oila360.uz/api/v1 replacement for the legacy devices/{dsn}/applications/usage. The child device authenticates by bearer deviceToken and reports usage deltas here (OilaDeviceAPI.reportAppUsage). Added so the coverage gate reflects the live endpoint the app actually calls.

Response Update Device Global Application Lock Api Members Device  Id  Global Application Lock Patch

post.description:
Create a daily full-device lock schedule for a child device owned by the parent.

Example: start_time=22:00, end_time=07:00 locks overnight (crosses midnight).

Example: start_time=08:00, end_time=18:00 locks during the day.

Example: start_time=end_time locks for the whole day (24/7).

delete.description: Delete full-device lock schedule for a child device owned by the parent.
